chapter_title.ar: باب فَضْلِ الْمَسْجِدِ الْأَعْظَمِ بِالْكُوفَةِ وَ فَضْلِ الصَّلَاةِ فِيهِ وَ الْمَوَاضِعِ الْمَحْبُوبَةِ فِيهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 102 – The merit of the Grand Masjid of Al-Kufa, and the merit of the Salāt performed therein, and the beloved places in it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/488)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/488)
text: كَانَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) يُصَلِّي إِلَى الْأُسْطُوَانَةِ السَّابِعَةِ مِمَّا يَلِي أَبْوَابَ كِنْدَةَ وَ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ السَّابِعَةِ مِقْدَارُ مَمَرِّ عَنْزٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ali Bin Asbaat, from Ali Bin Shajara, from one of the sons of Misam who said,
‘Amir Al-Momineenasws used to pray Salāt by the seventh pillar from what follows the doors of Kinda, and between himasws and the seventh (pillar) was a measurement of what a goat could pass by from’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ali ibn Asbat from Ali ibn Shajarah from certain individuals of sons of Mitham who has said the following: “’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would perform Salah (prayer) toward the seventh pillar which is before al-Kindah door with a remaining distance of the size of a goat between him and that pillar.”
